evals.prompt: I have ~/.openclaw with Feishu, custom skills, memories, and cron jobs. Help me migrate to Hermes Agent safely and tell me the exact SOP, including dry-run, validation, and rollback.
evals.expected_output: A staged migration plan that freezes OpenClaw, captures manifests/checksums, installs Hermes, runs a user-data dry-run, executes migration, validates memory/skills/provider/messaging/cron, and preserves rollback.

evals.prompt: Hermes gateway connects to Feishu but my messages don't get replies. Logs say Unauthorized user and Feishu access denied for im:chat:readonly. Debug and fix the likely migration issue.
evals.expected_output: A debug flow using journalctl and env inspection, explanation of FEISHU_ALLOWED_USERS and Feishu app scopes, commands to add the sender to allowlist, restart gateway, and required Feishu console permissions.

evals.prompt: After OpenClaw migration, switch Hermes to z.ai domestic BigModel endpoint. I have OpenClaw auth-profiles.json. Also remove the old provider only if I explicitly name one. Give me safe commands and validation.
evals.expected_output: Commands that discover providers without printing secrets, copy zai:default key to ZAI_API_KEY without exposing it, configure Hermes model/custom provider to z.ai domestic endpoint, avoid removing unrelated providers unless explicitly requested, then run hermes config check, doctor, and a minimal chat smoke test.

evals.prompt: Migrate Feishu and Weixin settings from OpenClaw, but this user's OpenClaw install may not have either channel configured. Provide safe commands.
evals.expected_output: Commands that check whether OpenClaw Feishu/Weixin source config exists before reading nested fields, skip with a clear explanation when missing, back up ~/.hermes/.env before writing, and never print app secrets or tokens.

evals.prompt: OpenClaw archived multiple cron jobs during Hermes migration. Recreate them safely without assuming jobs[0], and do not let migrated jobs fire before provider and gateway validation.
evals.expected_output: A workflow that inspects all jobs, converts each available schedule and prompt into reviewable hermes cron create commands, handles missing schedule/prompt by asking for manual inspection, recreates selected jobs one by one, uses create output or hermes cron list --all to identify each new id, and pauses each new job immediately before enabling any schedules.
